qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Items D + H from codex triage of prior cycle 20260519-151734: (D) /allow PreTool/PostTool asymmetry — substring grants bypass PreTool gates but are never consumed at PostTool, leaking past single-use; (H) PostToolUse:Agent bookmark inference races on parallel TodoWrite+Agent dispatch, flipping the wrong canonical step or no-writing.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: K=3 bounded-window is the AC-spec'd value. Codex (adversarial) noted that 4+ same-type parallel subagents within the same window would exceed K=3 and produce a no-match. This is the contracted behavior per AC-H1 (K=3) and AC-H3b (no over-anchor / no fallback start=0). Out-of-scope for this cycle; could be revisited as a follow-up enhancement if real workflows surface 4+ identical-type same-window dispatches.

Codex flagged: tightening PreTool literal match from exact_or_substr → exact_only may break user ergonomics if any documented or undocumented /allow workflow relied on substring matches (e.g. typing /allow Re to match Read). This is explicitly the fix's intent per AC-D1 — the security tightening is the deliverable. Existing users storing canonical tool names are unaffected. Mark for release-note communication if applicable; not a QA blocker.

Codex flagged sys.exit(0) as the Case A/Case B mutual-exclusion mechanism — observation only. The pattern is correct given that the hook's contract is 'tracking only, never blocks' (exit code 0 always) and Path A/B/Case A/Case B are mutually exclusive control-flow branches that all converge on sys.exit(0). Refactor into a structured return shape would improve unit-testability but is not a correctness issue.

qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: AC-D fix at hooks/lib/allowlist.py:144 switches read_grant's literal_policy from 'exact_or_substr' to 'exact_only' (added enum branch at line 91-92), aligning PreTool with PostTool consume_grant_for_posttool Branch 3 exact-only semantics. AC-H fix at hooks/posttool-subagent-track.py adds bounded-window K=3 typed lookup (_find_earliest_unflipped_matching_step lines 153-212), multi-in_progress guard (_current_in_progress_index returns None on >1 in_progress, lines 128-136), and contract-presence-FIRST restructure of _main (lines 494-505) preventing legacy fall-through from bypassing contract reconciliation.

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: git diff HEAD; direct read; grep for function symbols

qa.overall_assessment: PASS. All 10 acceptance criteria verified via independent smoke tests (27 pytest + 12 AC-D + 15 AC-H + 12 codex-adversarial-response = 66 total checks, all passing). No changes to out-of-scope files (pretool-read-size-guard.py, pretool-block-enterworktree.sh, pretool-todo-validate.py, settings.json all clean per git diff). No new permissions required. Codex adversarial consultation returned 9 findings; 5 actionable findings addressed with concrete new smoke tests, 4 are observation-only (K=3 bounded window is AC-spec'd, sys.exit branch style, ergonomics tradeoff, identity-weak subagent_type matching is the AC's contracted shape). All actionable codex findings demonstrate the implementation is correct: list-shape table-tests (5a-5f), PreTool/PostTool symmetry across 15 (tool, pattern) cases, E2E real-stdin invocation exit=0, identical-type parallel out-of-order (Codex-9a/9b/9c), contract gate safe-fallback.

success_criteria_results.evidence: AC-H3b(1a/1b/2/3/4) all pass. AC-H3b mutual-exclusion: Case A exits before Case B (no fall-through).

qa._success_criteria_rule: 10 ACs from acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json (AC-D1 through AC-D-H). All 10 verified.

root_cause_verification.rationale: Item D root cause: PreTool used exact_or_substr while PostTool Branch 3 used exact_only → substring grants bypassed PreTool but never consumed at PostTool. Fix: align PreTool to exact_only (hooks/lib/allowlist.py:149) — symmetry achieved (verified Codex-7 across 15 tool/pattern combinations). Item H root cause: bookmark in_progress inference assumed serial TodoWrite-then-Agent shape; parallel dispatch left bookmark stale. Fix: deterministic subagent_type keyed K=3 lookup, gated on single-ip anchor, contract-presence-FIRST routing. Both fixes target the documented root causes precisely.

build_verification.build_output_summary: Hook changes are loaded by Claude Code runtime on next invocation; no build/deploy needed. All 3 modified Python files pass py_compile syntax check.
build_verification.skip_reason: Hook code is loaded directly by Claude Code runtime — no compile or deploy step required. Syntax verified via python3 -m py_compile on all 3 modified files (allowlist.py, posttool-subagent-track.py, test_allowlist_consolidation.py). E2E real-stdin invocation of posttool-subagent-track.py runs cleanly (exit code 0).

regression_test_results.details: 27/27 tests pass in 0.04s. Includes 25 baseline + 2 new AC-D1 regression tests (test_read_grant_exact_only_rejects_substring, test_read_grant_exact_only_rejects_write_against_todowrite). No regressions.

codex_consult.channel: Skill(codex) — additional invocation after bash-exec codex
codex_consult.feedback_summary: Skill(codex) returned 3 findings: (H-1) IN_SCOPE_FINDING BLOCKER — claims posttool-subagent-track.py's _record_subagent_call_legacy and posttool-todo-sequence.py::save_state form a read-modify-write race that could clobber subagent_calls. (D-1) NO_IN_SCOPE_FINDING — read_grant exact_only correctness confirmed by codex. (OBS-1) OBSERVATION_ONLY — H regression harness lives at /var/tmp/dev-test/test_h.py not under hooks/tests/. Earlier bash-channel codex also produced 9 findings; 5 actionable addressed with 12 new smoke tests, 4 observation-only.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Skill(codex) H-1 classified as OUT_OF_SCOPE per /codex Rule 3: (a) the race is theoretical — Claude Code serializes tools within a response, so PostToolUse:TodoWrite and PostToolUse:Agent do not run concurrently for the same tool-pair dispatch; (b) the AC-H1 wording contracts only on subagent_calls[str(idx_N)] being set after PostToolUse:Agent fires — it does NOT contract on cross-hook lock semantics; (c) the load-modify-write pattern in posttool-todo-sequence.py::save_state pre-exists this cycle and is unmodified by it; (d) AC-H4 explicitly protects pretool-todo-validate.py and related sequence/validate files from change — touching save_state to add cross-hook locks would VIOLATE the cycle's regression scope. The finding is surfaced to the user as 'codex also identified one cross-hook lock concern outside the cycle scope — separate cycle?' OBS-1 acknowledged as observation; harness path /var/tmp/dev-test/test_h.py is non-durable but does not invalidate the in-tree pytest at hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py which carries the AC-D regression tests. D-1 explicitly NO_IN_SCOPE_FINDING — confirms PASS for all 5 AC-D criteria. Earlier bash-channel codex's 5 actionable findings remain addressed (12 new smoke tests). Final 4-layer evidence count (66 PASS / 0 FAIL) and verdict PASS unchanged.
